extraction.one_line_summary: VINGEGAARD attacked on Tourmalet descent, caught and dropped ROGLIC, won by 33 seconds over KUSS who defended red jersey
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma controlled from OBisque start with GESINK pacing, neutralizing attacks before systematic Tourmalet deployment showing defensive dominance converts to offense
EVENEPOEL cracked on first climb OBisque not third climb, suggesting high-altitude or early pace acceleration vulnerability rather than late-stage fatigue weakness
VINGEGAARD attacked Tourmalet descent not summit, exploiting technical terrain where descending skill amplifies power advantage over pure climbers
KUSS limited losses to 30 seconds despite being distanced, demonstrating team leader can survive teammates' attacks when pace controlled earlier

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: dropped on OBisque (first climb) losing 24+ minutes, indicating catastrophic form collapse or illness not tactical miscalculation
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: attacked descent after Tourmalet summit, caught teammate ROGLIC, won stage suggesting superior form to Tour de France peak
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: defended red jersey losing only 30 seconds to attacking teammate, showing unexpected resilience against world-class climbers
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: moved to third overall but caught by VINGEGAARD on descent, revealing descending remains tactical weakness despite climbing strength
rider_signals.RODRIGUEZ Cristian: positive: early break attempt before OBisque targeting KOM points suggests DSM using aggressive positioning before GC selection happens
